skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
√((x − 3)²) = √(5x + 21)

What is the smallest solution to the given equation?
rationale: Squaring both sides gives (x − 3)² = 5x + 21. Expanding: x² − 6x + 9 = 5x + 21, so x² − 11x − 12 = 0. Factoring: (x − 12)(x + 1) = 0, so x = 12 or x = −1. Both satisfy 5x + 21 ≥ 0. The smallest solution is −1.
